scope: Coheronia v0.6: ancestry/world-builder clarity, character-owned inventory, openable inventory panel, plant support rule, first differentiated tools.
summary: Executes the six-wave v0.6 work order. Shell: character creation shows a compact data-driven ancestry panel (description, live effects, tradeoffs, spawn band, biome affinities; planned label for non-live ancestries) and the world builder explains presets (description + deviations), sizes (dimensions), all six difficulty axes, and every generation slider from a new ui_help data section. Ownership boundary: carried inventory, hotbar slot, and tool tiers move to the character record in shell.json and travel between worlds; role starter items grant once per character; world saves keep terrain, hall, time, threats, storms, progression, and player position/health; legacy characters adopt a pre-v0.6 world save's player state once. New I-key inventory panel lists all carried stacks and tool state, mutually exclusive with the town panel, layered under Esc. Berry bushes require solid support: mining the support harvests the bush, load-time sweeps clean floaters without item side effects, and regrowth waits for support. Tools: blocks gained preferred_tool; an axe forged at the hall (4 wood + 2 stone) mines wood/plants 1.4x faster while stone/ore and the tier-2 pick gate are unchanged; tool state persists as character-owned {pick, axe} with legacy migration to axe 0 (must craft).
acceptance: validate_repo.py covers descriptions, ui_help, requires_support, preferred_tool, craft_axe -> RESULT scaffold_valid
Dwarf detail text contains mining bonus and constraint; non-live ancestry shows planned label; all six axis help entries present; dark_frontier deviations non-empty
Two characters keep distinct inventories in the same world; inventory survives a second world; role items never duplicate; legacy character migrates cleanly
toggle_inventory (I) bound and panel opens/closes/reflects counts; town panel behavior intact
Mining a bush's support removes the bush with food drops; save/load resurrects no floating bush; regrowth into unsupported air re-schedules
Baseline mining frames unchanged (dirt 21 / wood 33 / stone 66); axe cuts wood in 24 frames; stone unaffected; {pick, axe} round-trips; legacy tier migrates to axe 0
Smoke suite 122/122 (was 90), exit 0, at every wave gate
validation: python scripts/validate_repo.py -> RESULT scaffold_valid (baseline, every wave, closeout)
COHERONIA_SMOKE=1 waited windowed runs -> 94/94 (A/D), 106/106 (B/C), 122/122 (E/F + orchestrator fix)
decisions: Player XP/level stay world-owned in v0.6; documented in HANDOFF and VARIABLE_MATRIX for a future cross-world-leveling decision
No world save version bump: retired player keys degrade safely via .get defaults; ACCEPTED_VERSIONS unchanged
Axe is craft-only: new characters and legacy migrations both start at axe 0 (orchestrator corrected an agent brief that granted legacy characters a free axe)
requires_support and preferred_tool are generic block fields so future plants/tools inherit the rules without code branches
Load-time unsupported-bush cleanup grants no drops to avoid inventory side effects during load; mining the support does drop
Three sequential agents matched the work order's commit split because all waves share smoke_test.gd
risks: Smoke mutates the real user://shell.json; interrupted runs may leave test characters
Inventory panel read-only; axe single-tier; feel/tuning untested by human play
next_action: Operator playthrough; then farming + crafting menu, then research bench MVP.
